hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext:
[antigravity plugin] You have the `agy_delegate` and `agy_job` tools. Load the `antigravity` skill for full routing policy. Quick triggers — USE `agy_delegate` when the user asks you to:
- Scaffold/generate boilerplate, project structure, or stub modules
- Generate tests (unit, integration, edge-case) — you define the contract, agy writes
- Review code for bugs, security, or performance (first pass)
- Search the web or internal docs (tools you lack natively)
- Research a topic across multiple sources
- Migrate or refactor across many files
- Read/analyze large files and return a compact digest
- Any bulk, parallel, or repetitive work where offloading saves context

DON'T delegate single-line edits, simple questions, or judgement calls — the round-trip overhead exceeds the savings on tiny tasks.

COST DISCIPLINE:
- Always request digest output (set `digest: true`) — never paste agy's raw output back into the thread
- Use `dir` so agy reads real files instead of pasted context
- Batch into one large delegation over many small round-trips
- In one-shot/headless mode, run delegations synchronously

VERIFY (non-negotiable): never trust agy's self-reported "GREEN." Re-run the gate yourself in a clean state — agy has been observed patching installed packages and mock-stubbing deps to force a pass. You own correctness.

Turn this notice off via the plugin's `coding_policy` option.
